title: Story of Your Life
logline: When mysterious alien ships arrive on Earth, a linguist is recruited to decipher their language and discovers that learning their non-linear communication fundamentally alters her perception of time and memory.
antagonistOrConflict: The challenge of communicating with aliens whose non-linear language reveals the predetermined nature of time and choice

actBreaks.description: Louise enters the alien ship for the first time and encounters the heptapods, launching the central communication challenge.

validation.ruling: Louise and Ian approach the alien ship for the first time, witnessing the portal open and entering the craft, which irreversibly changes the story direction from recruitment/preparation to direct alien contact and fundamentally raises the stakes.

actBreaks.description: Louise gains deeper understanding of the heptapod language and begins experiencing non-linear time perception as global tensions escalate toward war.

validation.ruling: Louise discovers Abbott is dead and learns the complete heptapod story spanning 2.9 billion years written around the stasis chamber walls, representing the ultimate revelation that transforms her understanding and completes her journey into non-linear time perception.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated circular structure where the opening scenes of Louise with her daughter Hannah are revealed to be flash-forwards rather than flashbacks. The narrative weaves between present-day first contact with aliens and glimpses of Louise's future life, creating a temporal puzzle that mirrors the heptapods' non-linear language. The structure itself becomes thematic, demonstrating how understanding their language fundamentally changes Louise's perception of time from linear to simultaneous.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains deliberate pacing with extended sequences in the alien ship (scenes 31-49) allowing for careful language development. Tension escalates through military pressure and global conflict fears, punctuated by increasingly frequent and longer flashforward sequences that initially confuse but gradually reveal their true nature as Louise's expanding temporal perception.

incitingIncident.description: Colonel Weber arrives at Louise's office, shuts down her computer, and recruits her for a translation mission involving the aliens, referencing her previous Farsi work for Army Intelligence.

validation.ruling: Colonel Weber arrives at Louise's office, shuts down her computer, and recruits her for alien translation work, shattering her ordinary world as a linguistics professor and forcing her into the main conflict of first contact.

midpoint.description: Louise asks Weber for an ultimatum extension while Ian demonstrates that Weber asked him to calculate force needed to break the ship's hull, which Ian refused to help with.

validation.ruling: Louise learns Weber is planning to attack the ship's hull and shifts from reactive translation work to proactive communication, asking the heptapods directly about the weapon while they respond that she and Ian are 'not ready' - a clear point of no return that raises personal stakes.

climax.description: Louise returns from her vision and interrupts the heated international argument to reveal that each nation has been given components to build a massive spacecraft, specifically naming what Britain, China, Peru, and Japan have received.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. “WAR ROOM” TENT - BACK TO SCENE

validation.ruling: Scene 99 shows setup and international arguing but not the peak confrontation. Scene 101 is the true climax where Louise interrupts the heated international argument with her newfound knowledge from the visions, revealing that each nation has components to build a massive spacecraft, directly resolving the main story question and demonstrating her completed character arc.

scenes.heading: EXT. STARFIELD IN SKY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A voiceover from Louise reflects on memory over a starfield.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise stands on her lake house deck with wine, gazing at stars while reflecting on time's constraints, as a man approaches out of focus.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise cradles newborn Hannah in a hospital as the baby wraps her tiny hand around Louise's finger.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACKYARD - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Four-year-old Hannah dressed as a cowgirl giggles while playing on a toy horse and pointing finger-guns.

scenes.heading: INT. HANNAH’S ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Eight-year-old Hannah tells Louise 'I love you' like a prayer while tucked in bed.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Twelve-year-old Hannah angrily tells Louise 'I hate you' and storms into her room.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise holds her dying fifteen-year-old daughter Hannah's hand in the hospital as the life monitor flatlines and Hannah's heart stops.
scenes.turningPointNote: The devastating loss of Louise's daughter establishes the emotional core and reveals the end of their relationship timeline

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera drifts down from blue sky through clouds to find a tree line and car on a road.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOUISE’S SEDAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Louise drives alone with file boxes in her backseat, looking older and hardened, wearing no wedding ring.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNIVERSITY CAMPUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Louise parks at the university as F-18 fighters slice across the sky overhead.

scenes.heading: EXT. STUDENT CENTER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise crosses campus where students huddle around a TV while more fighter jets rocket past overhead.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSITY CLASSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Louise begins teaching her linguistics class when students' phones buzz with alerts about CNN footage, disrupting the lesson with chatter and distraction.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S SEDAN - EVENING
scenes.summary: Louise drives home listening to radio reports about an unidentified object while passing street accidents and distressed people.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOUISE’S HOUSE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Louise's sedan pulls up to her lake house.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Louise sits alone on her couch with wine, watching TV coverage of alien landings in her sparsely decorated home with no family photos.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise wakes with a start from sleep, spooning extra pillows as if they were a bedfellow.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNIVERSITY CAMPUS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise returns to an eerily empty university campus with only two students hurrying between buildings.

scenes.heading: INT. UNIVERSITY CLASSROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise enters her empty classroom to find no students and a note on the blackboard reading 'ALIENS. SEE YOU NEXT WEEK.'

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S OFFICE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Colonel Weber arrives at Louise's office, shuts down her computer, and recruits her for a translation mission involving the aliens, referencing her previous Farsi work for Army Intelligence.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louise is recruited by the military, launching her into the central alien communication mission

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S BEDROOM - DAWN
scenes.summary: Louise wakes to the sound of a helicopter landing on her lawn.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Weber tests Louise with a Sanskrit word at her door before she boards the military helicopter that has landed on her lawn.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACKHAWK HELICOPTER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise boards the helicopter and meets physicist Ian Donnelly, who quotes from a book about language being the foundation of civilization.

scenes.heading: EXT. MONTANA LANDING SITE - DAY
scenes.summary: The helicopter approaches the Montana landing site where a massive alien sphere dwarfs the wilderness and emits a low drone that resonates in everyone's sternum.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - DAY
scenes.summary: The spherical alien ship towers over a field camp of military tents, appearing majestic and ominous with no discernible front or back.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACKHAWK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Weber explains that portals align every thirteen hours to form a tunnel to meet the aliens, and reveals they are waiting for the team now.

scenes.heading: EXT. HELIPAD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The team is escorted from the helicopter as Lt. Marks briefs Weber on unknown elements found in ship fragments.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENT COMPLEX - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Lt. Marks leads the team toward a medical tent while the alien sphere looms overhead with an optical illusion of rolling toward them, vibrating their teeth with its thrum.

scenes.heading: INT. MEDICAL TENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Dr. Kettler prepares to inject Louise and Ian with immunizations in the medical tent surrounded by diagnostic equipment.

scenes.heading: INT. “CLEAN ROOM” - DAY
scenes.summary: Lt. Marks helps Louise and Ian into HAZMAT suits while explaining there's a glass wall preventing physical contact with the aliens.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise and Ian enter the operations tent in HAZMAT suits where Weber shows them a whiteboard tracking twelve countries' activities at separate alien ship sites.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE SHIP - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The team approaches the alien craft's single visible porthole, an eight-foot diameter opening in the otherwise smooth metallic surface.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The team enters a disorienting smooth tunnel that closes behind them as they advance toward the inner chamber, trapping them for forty-five minutes.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The team enters a symmetrical chamber bisected by transparent glass where Louise removes her helmet to show the aliens her human face, leading to first contact with the heptapods Abbott and Costello.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct contact with the aliens occurs when Louise removes her helmet, establishing the communication dynamic

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - EVENING
scenes.summary: The team exits the ship with Louise carrying a whiteboard showing new words 'MAN/WOMAN/EARTH' as Weber confronts her about teaching the aliens to read without authorization.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: In a flashback, Louise gets ready for a date while calling out instructions to young Hannah about bedtime rules.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise finds six-year-old Hannah drawing on the floor while watching TV, working on an illustration of her imagined Saturday morning cartoon.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - EVENING
scenes.summary: Louise stands alone outside base camp, looking confused as she watches Ian enter the operations tent.

scenes.heading: EXT. MONTANA WILDERNESS - EVENING
scenes.summary: Hundreds of civilians in vehicles make a pilgrimage to the landing site, amassing at the military barricade a mile from base camp.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE LINE - THAT MOMENT
scenes.summary: Weber surveys the growing civilian encampment stretching beyond sight while stadium lights illuminate the barricade perimeter.

scenes.heading: INT. APC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Weber meets with Sergeant Rutherford in an APC to discuss the inadequate perimeter as thousands of civilians gather outside the barricade.

scenes.heading: EXT. APC - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Rutherford warns Weber they can only hold the civilian crowd for three days before needing to push out or be pushed in.

scenes.heading: EXT. STARFIELD IN SKY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A shooting star with a long tail arcs across the starfield.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two Army privates on guard duty react nervously to the shooting star while Ian sits nearby sketching the alien ship with fascination.

scenes.heading: INT. TALK SHOW STUDIO SET - MORNING
scenes.summary: Talk show host Richard Riley rants on TV about government incompetence in handling first contact with the aliens.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise enters the operations tent where multiple screens show Riley's rant and foreign military forces while Weber discusses steering the communication approach based on other countries' methods.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise removes her HAZMAT mask during communication with the heptapods, leading to breakthrough progress as they begin learning each other's names and language symbols.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louise's decision to remove her mask creates a communication breakthrough and establishes deeper connection with the aliens

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - LATER
scenes.summary: The heptapods write 'MORE' in English, indicating they want to continue the session beyond the scheduled time limit.

scenes.heading: INT. HANNAH’S BEDROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: In a flashback, six-year-old Hannah asks Louise to read 'The Giving Tree' again, saying it's just as good the second time.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Louise holds a hunting knife while the heptapods display the word 'WEAPON' on the transparent wall, causing Costello to leave as Weber demands answers through the speakers.
scenes.turningPointNote: The introduction of the word 'weapon' creates tension and causes one heptapod to leave, escalating the stakes

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The operations tent erupts in chaotic debate about the heptapods' use of the word 'weapon' while Weber points to new 'BLACKOUT' labels on the international tracking board.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Hannah argues with Louise about being grounded for not doing chores, complaining she'll miss a movie at Riley's sleepover that will bond everyone for the night.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise stumbles and nearly falls in the operations tent, caught by Ian, while Weber watches suspiciously and orders her to see Dr. Kettler.

scenes.heading: INT. DR. KETTLER’S TRAILER - LATER
scenes.summary: Louise waits in Dr. Kettler's trailer with Private Lasky, who sits nervously bouncing his knee and glaring at her when called in.

scenes.heading: INT. DR. KETTLER’S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Dr. Kettler examines Louise with a pen light and gives her an injection, dismissing her collapse as overwork rather than radiation poisoning.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The base camp compound has doubled in size.

scenes.heading: EXT. CIVILIAN ENCAMPMENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A massive civilian encampment outside the barricade includes families around campfires and a group of young men with hunting rifles and handguns at a picnic table.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRACKS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise wakes up in the barracks next to Ian, who tells her he's starting to see the value of language and admits he's single, while Louise says good communication doesn't prevent being single.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise and Ian step outside to find the massive sphere spinning in place with a low thrumming sound before it suddenly stops.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL SUITE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Colonel Weber meets with Agent Halpern, General Stahl, and Chief of Staff Black, who criticize the civilian team for only teaching the aliens grade-school English.

scenes.heading: INT. BARRACKS - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise wakes to find Private Combs assigned as her escort around base camp and into the shell on Colonel Weber's orders.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise approaches the ship with Combs to find Ian waiting with Green Berets, soldiers building an armored bunker, and three tanks positioned toward the ship.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise, Ian, and Lt. Marks enter the interview chamber to find Abbott and Costello waiting with four new turrets mounted on their side of the barrier.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Four Green Berets stand guard as Ian introduces math concepts to the heptapods using new flatscreen equipment, while Lt. Marks orders him to ask about the weapon.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE SHIP - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise explains to Weber that the heptapods write by knowing their entire sentence before beginning, completing it in a loop, suggesting they know everything they'll say before speaking.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise asks Weber for an ultimatum extension while Ian demonstrates that Weber asked him to calculate force needed to break the ship's hull, which Ian refused to help with.
scenes.turningPointNote: Weber's ultimatum and revelation that the military is planning to attack the ship escalates the conflict to a critical point

scenes.heading: INT. HANNAH’S ROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Hannah complains to Louise about learning the terrible new word 'homework' and argues it's illegal for school to make demands on her time.

scenes.heading: EXT. STARFIELD IN SKY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Time-lapse sequence shows the starfield moving across the night sky.

scenes.heading: INT. MESS TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise eats alone with Private Lasky, who asks if she's scared the aliens are doing something to her, while Ian joins them looking exhausted from working on Weber's timeline project.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S LIVING ROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Louise watches The Thin Man on TV while Hannah observes her mother's pity party and tells Louise that people think she's a ball-buster but Hannah knows she's actually vulnerable.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP HOUSING PRE-FABS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise falters on her way to housing, wiping tears from her eyes, when Private Combs appears as her escort to get her inside before curfew.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSING PRE-FAB - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise enters her pre-fab to find Dr. Kettler and Colonel Weber waiting inside, who confront her about headaches and staring off, with Weber threatening to remove her from the project.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - MORNING
scenes.summary: Ian stands before Abbott and Costello with Lt. Marks and Green Berets, reluctantly using a new translator device programmed with Louise's work to communicate with the heptapods.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise confronts Ian outside the ship after he exits the tunnel, calling him a son of a bitch for using the translator device built from her work.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - MORNING
scenes.summary: Louise storms into the operations tent to confront Weber about replacing her with technology, while every TV screen shows violence and seven of eleven countries have gone to blackout status.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise follows Ian toward the ship as he explains the heptapods are saying humanity will either die off or thrive based on choices made with them, while they notice Private Lasky and Combs carrying a heavy bag into the ship.

scenes.heading: INT. “CLEAN ROOM” - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise argues with Weber and Ian about going into the ship after an unprovoked attack, insisting the aliens opened another portal because they want answers.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ian catches Louise heading toward the ship and insists on going with her to keep her safe, while Weber forbids them from entering without formal invitation before they're bathed in shimmering light.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW INTERVIEW CHAMBER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise and Ian enter a new interview chamber where Costello uses a working vocal translator to ask why humans used weapons against them, while Louise attempts to explain it wasn't them.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct confrontation with the heptapods about human aggression, with working vocal translation enabling deeper communication

scenes.heading: EXT. STARFIELD IN SKY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The camera tilts down from the starfield to find the operations tent below.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise, Ian, and new physicists work at computer workstations analyzing eighty thousand unique equations combined with logograms, while Weber stands nearby monitoring their progress.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S STUDY - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Hannah asks Louise for the technical term for a deal where both sides get something, seeking a word that sounds like competition but both sides win.

scenes.heading: INT. OPERATIONS TENT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise wakes up at her work table and Ian shows her a 3D animation of a jet engine process that creates room-temperature nuclear reaction, explaining it's what the heptapods have given them.

scenes.heading: INT. “WAR ROOM” PRE-FAB BUILDING - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise confronts Weber and Agent Halpern in the war room, explaining the heptapods' gift is one piece of twelve that forms a larger whole, while Halpern suggests they're contestants for a prize.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S STUDY - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Hannah returns as Louise suddenly realizes and shouts 'A non-zero-sum game!' - the term Hannah was seeking.

scenes.heading: INT. “WAR ROOM” TENT - BACK TO SCENE
scenes.summary: Weber questions Louise about her angle in getting everyone to share discoveries, while Louise, still reeling from affecting her own memory, says she just wants to go home.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise stands outside staring at the ship when the portal tunnel slides open invitingly, and she crosses the field and steps inside.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise enters the empty interview chamber, approaches the transparent boundary, and watches it dissipate completely when she touches it, allowing her to venture deeper into the ship.

scenes.heading: INT. CURVED HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise follows a light trail down a curved hallway to another door that opens like an aperture as she approaches.

scenes.heading: INT. STASIS CHAMBER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise discovers Abbott's body in a stasis chamber, missing arms and darkened, while Costello tells her through the translator that Abbott is dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Abbott's death is revealed as a direct consequence of the human attack, fundamentally changing Louise's understanding of the cost of human aggression

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The spherical ship powers up and the ground trembles.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW CHAMBER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise hurries past the consoles toward the exit of the interview chamber.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise runs full-tilt through the tunnel as ocular doors slide shut behind her and the tunnel ahead begins to constrict.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Louise runs out of the ship as the portal closes behind her, stumbles on shaking ground, and watches the massive sphere rise into the air and disappear into the stars.
scenes.turningPointNote: The ship's departure marks the end of direct contact with the heptapods and a major shift in the story's dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: A doctor pulls a sheet over Hannah's head in a hospital.

scenes.heading: EXT. CEMETERY - DAY (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Louise stands at Hannah's grave in a cemetery.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - BACK TO SCENE
scenes.summary: Louise comes out of the flashbacks back to present day, wobbles from their physical effect, and asks Ian to hang onto her before her eyes roll back.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S KITCHEN - DAY (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Louise stands at her refrigerator, abandons her task, and realizes she's still wearing her wedding ring as Hannah asks her to promise not to leave like Daddy did.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - BACK TO SCENE
scenes.summary: Louise recovers from her episode and tells Ian she remembered why her husband left her, surprising him with the revelation that she was married.

scenes.heading: INT. “WAR ROOM” TENT - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Louise and Ian enter the war room where Agent Halpern argues with other nations via video conference while Weber notes that forty-one thousand nuclear warheads are armed and in launch position.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation of global nuclear mobilization represents the peak of international crisis and the imminent threat of world war

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S LIVING ROOM - DAY (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: Louise naps on her couch surrounded by work papers when Hannah arrives with a delivery package and asks permission to open it.

scenes.heading: INT. “WAR ROOM” TENT - BACK TO SCENE
scenes.summary: Louise returns from her vision and interrupts the heated international argument to reveal that each nation has been given components to build a massive spacecraft, specifically naming what Britain, China, Peru, and Japan have received.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louise reveals the true purpose of the alien gifts - they are components for humanity's survival spacecraft

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Weber confronts Louise outside, amazed by how she knew classified information about what other nations received, and Louise simply explains that she 'remembered' it.

scenes.heading: INT. HANNAH’S ROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)
scenes.summary: In a flashback, Louise tucks four-year-old Hannah into bed and Hannah asks why she has her name, with Louise explaining it's special because it's a palindrome that reads the same forwards and backwards.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE - DAY
scenes.summary: Louise arrives at her lake house via military vehicle as she narrates Hannah's story in voiceover.

scenes.heading: INT. HAIR SALON - DAY
scenes.summary: Louise gets her hair styled at a salon while voiceover reveals the heptapods gave humanity a choice to survive, accompanied by quick shots of massive spaceship construction and launch preparations.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Louise stands before a mirror holding up a dress while in voiceover she reflects on making a choice she'll have to live with forever.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUISE’S FOYER - EVENING
scenes.summary: Ian arrives at Louise's house dressed up with wine for their date, and Louise appears with the short haircut matching the flashbacks.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE HOUSE BALCONY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Louise and Ian recreate the opening balcony scene shot-for-shot, revealing all the 'flashbacks' were actually visions of the future, as Ian asks Louise if she wants to make a baby.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that all flashbacks were actually future visions, and Louise faces the choice to have Hannah knowing her fate

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWER APARTMENT BALCONY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: An older Louise watches from her apartment balcony as the first ark ship launches into space, reflecting in voiceover that she wouldn't change anything because she didn't.
scenes.turningPointNote: Louise's final acceptance of her choice and humanity's departure to the stars
